answer:
The correct answer is \boxed{E}.  

Statement (i) is true because standardized residuals are often assumed to be normally distributed in GARCH estimation. Statement (iii) is true because financial data typically exhibit fat tails, so standardized residuals often still show excess kurtosis. Statements (ii) and (iv) are false: if the GARCH model is appropriate, squared standardized residuals should not show autocorrelation, and standardized residuals differ from raw residuals due to scaling by conditional volatility.
